name: Darshan Maniar
tagline: I take legacy systems everyone's afraid to touch and make them fast, stable, and modern.
summary: Full Stack Engineer with 7+ years in enterprise software: legacy modernization, performance work, and leading small teams. In 2025 I moved to Germany for an M.Sc. at Hochschule Heilbronn, because I'm here to build a career for the long run, not to collect a degree.

availability.now: working student, internship, or freelance — 20 h/week in semester, full-time in semester breaks.

stats.label: apps in the modernization program I led

journey.title: B.E. Computer Science
journey.detail: Where it all started. Graduated 2017 with a CGPA of 8.24 and an itch to build real things.

journey.title: Junior Software Engineer → Senior Software Engineer
journey.detail: Interned three months, then joined as a Junior Software Engineer that December. Promoted to Senior in 2020. Five years, one lesson: maintainable beats clever.

journey.title: Software Engineer III
journey.detail: Led legacy modernization for systems serving 1M+ members. Built monitoring that cut failure detection from 8-10 hours to 10-15 minutes.

journey.title: M.Sc. Software Engineering & Management
journey.detail: New country, new language, and a move I made on purpose. Germany is the long game; the degree is how I get in the door, not the reason I came.

journey.title: Full-time, somewhere good
journey.detail: Graduating early 2027. Until then: working student, internship, or freelance. After: building things that outlive their framework.

german.note: Deutsch lernen: in progress. Ich gebe nicht auf.

highlights: Architected a real-time health monitoring system (React, .NET microservices, Azure Workbooks) for 50+ global applications. Failure detection went from 8-10 hours to 10-15 minutes.
Stabilized a legacy subscription platform serving 50K vendors and 1M+ international members; support ticket volume fell by roughly a third over the following year.
Drove the TFS → Git migration, framework upgrades, and automated CI/CD across the legacy estate; deployment time fell from 2-3 hours to about one.
Built .NET 8 microservices with RabbitMQ and Web API to restructure member subscription processing.
Led PI planning and mentored 4-5 junior developers.

experience.position: Junior Software Engineer → Senior Software Engineer
highlights: Built an in-house Alexa skill end-to-end, front-end and back-end, during the 2018 voice-assistant wave.
Modernized a high-traffic, multi-role POS portal from AngularJS through a hybrid architecture to Angular; owned back-end services and stored procedures through peak summer seasons of 50K orders.
Rebuilt a rehab-facility ERP from classic ASP to Angular, managing enrollment, therapy scheduling, and billing to partner foundations for 300-400 concurrent patients.

Digitized a gene-testing lab's paper intake with a React front-end over a multi-stack back-end, and built the ETL pipeline feeding a long-term patient health-data store across 150-180 partner clinics.
Promoted to Senior in 2020; owned code review and pair programming for the team's junior developers from there on.

projects.title: System Health Monitoring Dashboard
projects.role: Senior Full Stack Developer · Reveation Labs
projects.pitch: From "the client called, something's down" to "we fixed it before anyone noticed."
points: Real-time dashboard and alerting across the 50+ applications running in production worldwide.
Mean time to detection: 8-10 hours → 10-15 minutes.
Alerts carry contextual error detail, so the on-call engineer starts at the cause instead of the symptom.

projects.title: Legacy Modernization & DevOps Transformation
projects.pitch: Turning a VB-era monolith into modular, CI/CD-powered components, without breaking production.
points: Migrated legacy VB applications from TFS to Git and upgraded .NET framework versions; the intensive migration phase ran Dec 2024 - Jan 2025.
Modular NuGet package strategy eliminated version chaos across the 200+ applications in the legacy estate.
CI/CD with automated code review and a manual testing gate, so releasing stopped depending on who was around to do it by hand.

projects.title: AI Tool Adoption in Academic Research
projects.role: Coursework research project · 1st semester · Hochschule Heilbronn
projects.pitch: What actually happens when researchers adopt AI tools? I surveyed, measured, and found out.
points: Designed and ran a structured survey: 36 responses over 29 days.
Descriptive statistics on the quantitative answers, hand-coded themes on the free text.
78% of respondents reported productivity gains, but reliability gaps were eroding their trust in the tools.

projects.title: This Site & the Always-Current CV
projects.pitch: The CV you download here is compiled from LaTeX by CI on every push, so it can never fall out of date.
points: React site with all content in a single JSON file, so copy changes never touch components.
GitHub Actions pipeline compiles English and German CVs from LaTeX on every push.
Open source. Read the code; it's the most honest skills section on this page.

languages.name: English
languages.level: fluent, my working language for 7+ years
